docs.title: Standards Catalog Framework
docs.text: A Go framework for creating, validating, and managing standards catalogs.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Standards Catalog Framework provides:

  • :material-file-document: Schema definitions for standards catalogs
  • :material-check-circle: Validation tools for catalog entries
  • :material-console: CLI tool for catalog management
  • :material-book: Go library for programmatic access
  • :material-graph: Web visualization for interactive exploration

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/cmd/standards-catalog@latest

docs.title: Create a Catalog
docs.text:
# catalog.yaml
apiVersion: standards-catalog/v1
kind: Catalog
metadata:
  name: my-standards
  version: "1.0.0"

standards:
  - id: oauth2
    name: OAuth 2.0
    version: "2.1"
    status: adopted
    organization: IETF
    specUrl: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1/
    category: authorization
    layer: human

docs.title: Validate and List
docs.text:
# Validate catalog
standards-catalog validate catalog.yaml

# List all standards
standards-catalog list catalog.yaml

# Generate visualization
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o standards-graph.html

docs.title: Ecosystem
docs.text:
Standards Catalog Framework (SCF)     ← You are here
        │
        ▼
Agent Standards Catalog (ASC)
        │
        ▼
agent-protocols (Go implementations)
Project Purpose Standards Catalog Framework Generic framework for any standards catalog Agent Standards Catalog Catalog of AI agent standards agent-protocols Go implementations of protocols

docs.title: Identity Layers
docs.text:
Standards are organized by identity layer:

Layer Color Description Examples Human :material-circle:{ style="color: #00d4ff" } Cyan Human identity and delegation OIDC, SAML, ID-JAG Agent :material-circle:{ style="color: #ff6b00" } Orange AI agent identity AAuth, A2A, MCP Workload :material-circle:{ style="color: #a855f7" } Purple Workload/service identity SPIFFE, WIMSE Service :material-circle:{ style="color: #22c55e" } Green Service-to-service auth OAuth 2.0, mTLS

docs.title: Categories
docs.text: Category Description authentication Identity verification authorization Access control decisions identity Identity representation provisioning Lifecycle management communication Protocol communication discovery Service/agent discovery governance Policy and compliance

docs.title: Status Values
docs.text: Status Description draft In development proposed Proposed for adoption adopted Widely adopted deprecated Deprecated but still used retired No longer used

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Detailed setup guide
  • CLI Reference - All CLI commands
  • Library Usage - Go library API
  • Visualization - Interactive standards graph

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project are documented here.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Unreleased
docs.text: No unreleased changes.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• export CLI command for JSON output
  • generate-web CLI command for interactive HTML visualization
  • Interactive D3.js visualization with graph and table views
  • MkDocs documentation site with Material theme
  • Filter controls for layer, organization, category, status
  • Color-coded identity layers in visualization

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • README updated with web visualization documentation

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release
  • Core catalog types: Catalog, Standard, Implementation
  • Enumeration types: StandardStatus, Category, IdentityLayer, ImplementationStatus
  • YAML and JSON file format support
  • Validation system with configurable options
  • CLI commands: validate, list, show, query, stats
  • Go library with query methods:
  • FindByID
  • FindByCategory
  • FindByLayer
  • FindByStatus
  • FindByTag
  • FindByOrganization
  • GetOrganizations
  • GetTags
  • Cross-reference validation for compatibleWith, supersedes, supersededBy
  • Example catalog in testdata/

docs.title: Validation Features
docs.text:
• Required field validation
  • ID format validation (lowercase, hyphens)
  • URL validation
  • Enum value validation
  • ID uniqueness validation
  • Cross-reference resolution

docs.title: CLI Features
docs.text:
• Tabular output for list and query
  • JSON output for show
  • Statistics by status, category, layer, organization
  • Filter flags for query command

For detailed release notes, see the Releases section.

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: The standards-catalog CLI provides commands for managing standards catalogs.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/cmd/standards-catalog@latest

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate a catalog file for correctness.

standards-catalog validate <catalog-file>

Arguments:

  • <catalog-file> - Path to YAML or JSON catalog file

Example:

$ standards-catalog validate catalog.yaml
Catalog 'AI Agent Standards' is valid (12 standards)

Validation checks:

  • Required fields present
  • ID format (lowercase, hyphens only)
  • URL validity
  • Enum values (status, category, layer)
  • ID uniqueness
  • Cross-reference validity (compatibleWith, supersedes)

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all standards in a catalog.

standards-catalog list <catalog-file>

Example:

$ standards-catalog list catalog.yaml
ID        NAME                  VERSION  STATUS   CATEGORY        LAYER
--        ----                  -------  ------   --------        -----
oauth2    OAuth 2.0             2.1      adopted  authorization   human
oidc      OpenID Connect        1.0      adopted  authentication  human
aauth     AAuth Protocol        02       draft    authorization   agent
spiffe    SPIFFE                1.0      adopted  identity        workload

docs.title: show
docs.text:
Show details of a specific standard as JSON.

standards-catalog show <catalog-file> <standard-id>

Arguments:

  • <catalog-file> - Path to catalog file
  • <standard-id> - ID of the standard to show

Example:

$ standards-catalog show catalog.yaml oauth2
{
  "id": "oauth2",
  "name": "OAuth 2.0",
  "version": "2.1",
  "status": "adopted",
  "organization": "IETF",
  "specUrl": "https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1/",
  "category": "authorization",
  "layer": "human",
  "description": "Industry-standard protocol for authorization",
  "protocols": ["HTTP", "TLS"],
  "tags": ["authorization", "foundational"]
}

docs.title: query
docs.text:
Query standards with filters.

standards-catalog query <catalog-file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description Example --category Filter by category --category=authorization --layer Filter by identity layer --layer=agent --status Filter by status --status=adopted --org Filter by organization --org=IETF --tag Filter by tag --tag=foundational

Examples:

# Find all agent-layer standards
standards-catalog query catalog.yaml --layer=agent

# Find IETF authorization standards
standards-catalog query catalog.yaml --org=IETF --category=authorization

# Find adopted standards
standards-catalog query catalog.yaml --status=adopted

docs.title: stats
docs.text:
Show catalog statistics.

standards-catalog stats <catalog-file>

Example:

$ standards-catalog stats catalog.yaml
Catalog: AI Agent Standards (v1.0.0)
Total standards: 12

By Status:
  adopted: 5
  draft: 6
  proposed: 1

By Category:
  authorization: 4
  authentication: 3
  identity: 2
  communication: 2
  provisioning: 1

By Layer:
  human: 3
  agent: 5
  workload: 2
  service: 2

Organizations: IETF, OpenID Foundation, CNCF, Google, Anthropic
Tags: authorization, authentication, identity, agent, foundational

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export catalog as formatted JSON.

standards-catalog export <catalog-file>

Example:

# Export to stdout
standards-catalog export catalog.yaml

# Export to file
standards-catalog export catalog.yaml > catalog.json

docs.title: generate-web
docs.text:
Generate an interactive HTML visualization.

standards-catalog generate-web <catalog-file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Short Description Default --output -o Output HTML file path standards-graph.html

Example:

# Generate with default filename
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml

# Generate with custom filename
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o docs/visualization.html

Features of generated visualization:

  • Force-directed graph view
  • Table view
  • Filter by layer, organization, category, status
  • Color-coded by identity layer
  • Click nodes to open specifications
  • Zoom and pan support

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (validation failed, file not found, etc.)

docs.title: File Formats
docs.text:
The CLI auto-detects format by file extension:

Extension Format .yaml, .yml YAML .json JSON

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through creating your first standards catalog.

docs.title: CLI Tool
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/cmd/standards-catalog@latest

docs.title: Go Library
docs.text: go get github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework

docs.title: Step 1: Create the Catalog File
docs.text:
Create a file named catalog.yaml:

apiVersion: standards-catalog/v1
kind: Catalog
metadata:
  name: my-standards-catalog
  version: "1.0.0"
  description: My collection of standards
  maintainers:
    - name: Your Name
      email: you@example.com

standards: []

docs.title: Step 2: Add Standards
docs.text:
Add standards to the standards array:

standards:
  - id: oauth2
    name: OAuth 2.0
    version: "2.1"
    status: adopted
    organization: IETF
    specUrl: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1/
    category: authorization
    layer: human
    description: Industry-standard protocol for authorization
    protocols:
      - HTTP
      - TLS
    tags:
      - authorization
      - foundational

  - id: oidc
    name: OpenID Connect
    version: "1.0"
    status: adopted
    organization: OpenID Foundation
    specUrl: https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html
    category: authentication
    layer: human
    description: Identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0
    compatibleWith:
      - oauth2
    tags:
      - authentication
      - identity

docs.title: Step 3: Validate
docs.text:
standards-catalog validate catalog.yaml

Expected output:

Catalog 'my-standards-catalog' is valid (2 standards)

docs.title: Step 4: Explore
docs.text:
# List all standards
standards-catalog list catalog.yaml

# Show statistics
standards-catalog stats catalog.yaml

# Query by layer
standards-catalog query catalog.yaml --layer=human

# Show specific standard
standards-catalog show catalog.yaml oauth2

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
Every standard must have these fields:

Field Description Example id Unique identifier (lowercase, hyphens) oauth2, id-jag name Human-readable name OAuth 2.0 version Standard version 2.1, RFC 7519 status Maturity status draft, adopted organization Issuing body IETF, CNCF specUrl Link to specification https://... category Functional category authorization layer Identity layer human, agent

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: Field Description description Brief description of the standard protocols Related protocols (HTTP, TLS, gRPC) implementations Known implementations compatibleWith IDs of compatible standards supersedes IDs of superseded standards supersededBy IDs of standards that supersede this tags Arbitrary labels for filtering metadata Custom key-value pairs

docs.title: Adding Implementations
docs.text:
Document known implementations:

standards:
  - id: spiffe
    name: SPIFFE
    # ... required fields ...
    implementations:
      - name: SPIRE
        url: https://github.com/spiffe/spire
        status: stable
      - name: go-spiffe
        url: https://github.com/spiffe/go-spiffe
        language: Go
        status: stable

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
Standards that work together:

- id: oidc
  compatibleWith:
    - oauth2

docs.title: Succession
docs.text:
When one standard replaces another:

- id: oauth2-v3
  supersedes:
    - oauth2

docs.title: Generating Visualization
docs.text:
Create an interactive HTML visualization:

standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o standards-graph.html

Open standards-graph.html in a browser to explore your catalog visually.

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
Catalogs can also be written in JSON:

{
  "apiVersion": "standards-catalog/v1",
  "kind": "Catalog",
  "metadata": {
    "name": "my-standards-catalog",
    "version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "standards": [
    {
      "id": "oauth2",
      "name": "OAuth 2.0",
      "version": "2.1",
      "status": "adopted",
      "organization": "IETF",
      "specUrl": "https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1/",
      "category": "authorization",
      "layer": "human"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• CLI Reference - Complete command documentation
  • Library Usage - Use the Go library
  • Schema Reference - Full schema details

docs.title: Library Usage
docs.text: The Go library provides programmatic access to standards catalogs.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework

docs.title: Load Only
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/catalog"
)

func main() {
    c, err := catalog.Load("catalog.yaml")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Loaded %d standards\n", len(c.Standards))
}

docs.title: Load and Validate
docs.text:
c, errors, err := catalog.LoadAndValidate("catalog.yaml")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

if errors.HasErrors() {
    for _, e := range errors {
        fmt.Printf("Validation error: %s\n", e)
    }
    os.Exit(1)
}

docs.title: Find by ID
docs.text:
standard := c.FindByID("oauth2")
if standard != nil {
    fmt.Printf("Found: %s\n", standard.Name)
}

docs.title: Find by Layer
docs.text:
agentStandards := c.FindByLayer(catalog.LayerAgent)
for _, s := range agentStandards {
    fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", s.ID, s.Name)
}

docs.title: Find by Category
docs.text: authStandards := c.FindByCategory(catalog.CategoryAuthorization)

docs.title: Find by Status
docs.text: adoptedStandards := c.FindByStatus(catalog.StatusAdopted)

docs.title: Find by Organization
docs.text: ietfStandards := c.FindByOrganization("IETF")

docs.title: Find by Tag
docs.text: foundationalStandards := c.FindByTag("foundational")

docs.title: Get All Organizations
docs.text:
orgs := c.GetOrganizations()
fmt.Printf("Organizations: %v\n", orgs)
// Output: Organizations: [CNCF IETF OpenID Foundation]

docs.title: Get All Tags
docs.text:
tags := c.GetTags()
fmt.Printf("Tags: %v\n", tags)

docs.title: Basic Validation
docs.text:
validator := catalog.NewValidator()
errors := validator.Validate(c)

if errors.HasErrors() {
    for _, e := range errors {
        fmt.Println(e)
    }
}

docs.title: Custom Validation Options
docs.text:
validator := catalog.NewValidator(
    catalog.WithRequireImplementations(true),
    catalog.WithRequireDescription(true),
    catalog.WithAllowedOrganizations([]string{"IETF", "CNCF"}),
    catalog.WithAllowedCategories([]catalog.Category{
        catalog.CategoryAuthorization,
        catalog.CategoryAuthentication,
    }),
    catalog.WithAllowedLayers([]catalog.IdentityLayer{
        catalog.LayerAgent,
        catalog.LayerHuman,
    }),
)

errors := validator.Validate(c)

docs.title: Save as YAML
docs.text: err := catalog.Save(c, "output.yaml")

docs.title: Save as JSON
docs.text: err := catalog.Save(c, "output.json")

docs.title: Standard Status
docs.text:
const (
    StatusDraft      StandardStatus = "draft"
    StatusProposed   StandardStatus = "proposed"
    StatusAdopted    StandardStatus = "adopted"
    StatusDeprecated StandardStatus = "deprecated"
    StatusRetired    StandardStatus = "retired"
)

docs.title: Categories
docs.text:
const (
    CategoryAuthentication Category = "authentication"
    CategoryAuthorization  Category = "authorization"
    CategoryIdentity       Category = "identity"
    CategoryProvisioning   Category = "provisioning"
    CategoryCommunication  Category = "communication"
    CategoryDiscovery      Category = "discovery"
    CategoryGovernance     Category = "governance"
)

docs.title: Identity Layers
docs.text:
const (
    LayerHuman    IdentityLayer = "human"
    LayerAgent    IdentityLayer = "agent"
    LayerWorkload IdentityLayer = "workload"
    LayerService  IdentityLayer = "service"
)

docs.title: Implementation Status
docs.text:
const (
    ImplStatusExperimental ImplementationStatus = "experimental"
    ImplStatusBeta         ImplementationStatus = "beta"
    ImplStatusStable       ImplementationStatus = "stable"
    ImplStatusDeprecated   ImplementationStatus = "deprecated"
)

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/catalog"
)

func main() {
    // Load and validate
    c, errors, err := catalog.LoadAndValidate("catalog.yaml")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    if errors.HasErrors() {
        log.Fatal(errors)
    }

    // Print statistics
    fmt.Printf("Catalog: %s v%s\n", c.Metadata.Name, c.Metadata.Version)
    fmt.Printf("Total standards: %d\n\n", len(c.Standards))

    // Group by layer
    layers := []catalog.IdentityLayer{
        catalog.LayerHuman,
        catalog.LayerAgent,
        catalog.LayerWorkload,
        catalog.LayerService,
    }

    for _, layer := range layers {
        standards := c.FindByLayer(layer)
        fmt.Printf("%s layer (%d):\n", layer, len(standards))
        for _, s := range standards {
            fmt.Printf("  - %s (%s)\n", s.Name, s.Status)
        }
    }

    // Export specific standard as JSON
    if s := c.FindByID("oauth2"); s != nil {
        data, _ := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
        fmt.Printf("\nOAuth 2.0 details:\n%s\n", data)
    }
}

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Full API documentation is available at pkg.go.dev.

docs.title: Schema Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for the standards catalog schema.

docs.title: Catalog Structure
docs.text:
apiVersion: standards-catalog/v1  # Required
kind: Catalog                      # Required
metadata:                          # Required
  name: string                     # Required
  version: string                  # Required
  description: string              # Optional
  maintainers:                     # Optional
    - name: string                 # Required
      email: string                # Optional
      url: string                  # Optional
  lastUpdated: timestamp           # Optional
standards:                         # Required
  - <Standard>

docs.title: Standard
docs.text:
# Required fields
id: string              # Unique identifier
name: string            # Human-readable name
version: string         # Standard's version
status: StandardStatus  # Maturity status
organization: string    # Issuing body
specUrl: string         # URL to specification
category: Category      # Functional category
layer: IdentityLayer    # Identity layer

# Optional fields
description: string                 # Brief description
protocols: [string]                 # Related protocols
implementations: [Implementation]   # Known implementations
compatibleWith: [string]           # Compatible standard IDs
supersedes: [string]               # Superseded standard IDs
supersededBy: [string]             # Superseding standard IDs
tags: [string]                     # Arbitrary labels
metadata: {string: string}         # Custom key-value pairs

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
name: string                    # Required - Implementation name
url: string                     # Required - URL to implementation
language: string                # Optional - Programming language
status: ImplementationStatus    # Optional - Maturity status
description: string             # Optional - Additional details

docs.title: StandardStatus
docs.text: Value Description draft In development, not yet stable proposed Proposed for adoption, seeking feedback adopted Widely adopted and stable deprecated Deprecated, but still in use retired No longer used or maintained

docs.title: Category
docs.text: Value Description authentication Identity verification protocols authorization Access control and permissions identity Identity representation and management provisioning Lifecycle management (SCIM, etc.) communication Protocol communication (A2A, MCP) discovery Service/agent discovery mechanisms governance Policy and compliance standards

docs.title: IdentityLayer
docs.text: Value Description Examples human Human identity and delegation OIDC, SAML, ID-JAG agent AI agent identity AAuth, A2A, MCP workload Workload/service identity SPIFFE, WIMSE service Service-to-service auth OAuth 2.0, mTLS, JWT

docs.title: ImplementationStatus
docs.text: Value Description experimental Early stage, API may change beta Testing stage, approaching stability stable Production-ready deprecated No longer maintained

docs.title: ID Format
docs.text:
• Must start with a lowercase letter
  • Can contain lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
  • Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$

Valid: oauth2, id-jag, a2a, http-signatures

Invalid: OAuth2, ID-JAG, 2fa, -auth

docs.title: URL Fields
docs.text:
• specUrl must be a valid URL
  • Implementation url must be a valid URL

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• compatibleWith must reference existing standard IDs
  • supersedes must reference existing standard IDs
  • supersededBy must reference existing standard IDs

docs.title: Uniqueness
docs.text: • Standard id must be unique within the catalog

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
apiVersion: standards-catalog/v1
kind: Catalog
metadata:
  name: AI Agent Standards Catalog
  version: "1.0.0"
  description: Catalog of identity and authorization standards for AI agents
  maintainers:
    - name: John Wang
      email: john@example.com
      url: https://example.com
  lastUpdated: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z

standards:
  - id: oauth2
    name: OAuth 2.0
    version: "2.1"
    status: adopted
    organization: IETF
    specUrl: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1/
    category: authorization
    layer: human
    description: Industry-standard protocol for authorization
    protocols:
      - HTTP
      - TLS
    implementations:
      - name: golang-oauth2
        url: https://github.com/golang/oauth2
        language: Go
        status: stable
      - name: authlib
        url: https://github.com/lepture/authlib
        language: Python
        status: stable
    tags:
      - authorization
      - foundational
      - token
    metadata:
      rfc: "6749"
      maintainer: IETF OAuth WG

  - id: oidc
    name: OpenID Connect
    version: "1.0"
    status: adopted
    organization: OpenID Foundation
    specUrl: https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html
    category: authentication
    layer: human
    description: Identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0
    compatibleWith:
      - oauth2
    protocols:
      - HTTP
      - TLS
    tags:
      - authentication
      - identity
      - sso

  - id: aauth
    name: AAuth Protocol
    version: "02"
    status: draft
    organization: IETF
    specUrl: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-hardt-oauth-aauth-protocol/
    category: authorization
    layer: agent
    description: Agent authorization with mission-based consent
    compatibleWith:
      - id-jag
      - spiffe
    protocols:
      - HTTP
      - http-signatures
      - JWT
    implementations:
      - name: agent-protocols
        url: https://github.com/aistandardsio/agent-protocols
        language: Go
        status: beta
    tags:
      - agent
      - authorization
      - mission

docs.title: JSON Schema
docs.text: The catalog format can be validated against a JSON Schema. See the schema directory for schema files.

docs.title: Visualization
docs.text: Interactive web visualization for exploring standards catalogs.

docs.title: Live Demo
docs.text:
Interactive Features

  • Drag nodes to rearrange the graph
  • Scroll to zoom in/out
  • Click nodes to open specification URLs
  • Toggle between Graph and Table views
  • Filter by layer, organization, category, or status

docs.title: From CLI
docs.text:
# Generate HTML with embedded catalog data
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o standards-graph.html

docs.title: Custom Output Location
docs.text:
# Generate to docs folder for MkDocs
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o docs/standards-graph.html

docs.title: Graph View
docs.text:
The force-directed graph shows:

  • Nodes: Each standard as a colored circle
  • Links: Relationships between standards
  • Dashed lines: compatibleWith relationships
  • Solid red lines: supersedes relationships

docs.title: Color Coding
docs.text:
Standards are colored by identity layer:

Layer Color Human :material-circle:{ style="color: #00d4ff" } Cyan Agent :material-circle:{ style="color: #ff6b00" } Orange Workload :material-circle:{ style="color: #a855f7" } Purple Service :material-circle:{ style="color: #22c55e" } Green

docs.title: Table View
docs.text:
Toggle to Table view for a sortable list with columns:

  • Standard name (clickable link to spec)
  • Organization
  • Layer
  • Category
  • Status
  • Version

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
Use the sidebar filters to narrow down displayed standards:

  • Identity Layers: Human, Agent, Workload, Service
  • Organizations: IETF, CNCF, OpenID Foundation, etc.
  • Categories: Authorization, Authentication, Identity, etc.
  • Status: Draft, Proposed, Adopted, Deprecated, Retired

docs.title: Method 1: Direct HTML File
docs.text:
Copy the generated HTML to your docs/ folder:

standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o docs/standards-graph.html

Link to it in navigation or markdown:

[View Standards Graph](standards-graph.html)

docs.title: Method 2: Iframe Embed
docs.text:
Embed in any markdown page:

<iframe
    src="standards-graph.html"
    width="100%"
    height="700"
    style="border: 1px solid #2a2a3a; border-radius: 8px;">
</iframe>

docs.title: Method 3: Full Page
docs.text:
Create a page that links directly to the visualization:

# mkdocs.yml
nav:
  - Visualization: standards-graph.html

docs.title: Editing Colors
docs.text:
The visualization uses CSS variables. Edit the <style> section:

:root {
    --accent-human: #00d4ff;    /* Cyan for human layer */
    --accent-agent: #ff6b00;    /* Orange for agent layer */
    --accent-workload: #a855f7; /* Purple for workload layer */
    --accent-service: #22c55e;  /* Green for service layer */
}

docs.title: Modifying Data
docs.text:
The generated HTML contains embedded catalog data. To update:

  1. Regenerate with updated catalog: standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml
  2. Or edit the catalogData object in the HTML directly

docs.title: Loading External Data
docs.text:
Modify the HTML to load data from an external JSON file:

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    fetch('catalog.json')
        .then(response => response.json())
        .then(data => {
            Object.assign(catalogData, data);
            init();
        });
});

docs.title: Browser Support
docs.text:
The visualization requires a modern browser with JavaScript enabled:

  • Chrome/Edge 90+
  • Firefox 88+
  • Safari 14+

Uses D3.js v7 loaded from CDN.

docs.title: v0.1.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-06-28

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Generic Go framework for creating, validating, and managing standards catalogs
  • CLI tool for validating, querying, and displaying catalog data
  • Comprehensive validation system with configurable options

docs.title: Core Types
docs.text:
The framework provides a complete type system for standards catalogs:

  • Catalog - Collection of standards with metadata
  • Standard - Individual standard entry with comprehensive fields
  • CatalogMetadata - Name, version, description, maintainers
  • Implementation - Known implementations with language/status
  • Maintainer - Contact information

docs.title: Enum Types
docs.text:
Type-safe enums for consistent data:

Type Values StandardStatus draft, proposed, adopted, deprecated, retired Category authentication, authorization, identity, provisioning, communication, discovery, governance IdentityLayer human, agent, workload, service ImplementationStatus experimental, beta, stable, deprecated

docs.title: File I/O
docs.text:
• Load() - Load catalog from JSON/YAML file
  • Parse() - Parse raw bytes with format detection
  • Save() - Save catalog to file
  • LoadAndValidate() - Combined load and validate operation

docs.title: Query Methods
docs.text:
Filter and search standards programmatically:

  • FindByID() - Lookup standard by identifier
  • FindByCategory() - Filter by category
  • FindByLayer() - Filter by identity layer
  • FindByStatus() - Filter by status
  • FindByTag() - Filter by tags
  • FindByOrganization() - Filter by organization
  • GetOrganizations() - Get all unique organizations
  • GetTags() - Get all unique tags

docs.title: Validation System
docs.text:
Comprehensive validation with detailed error reporting:

  • Required field validation
  • ID format validation (lowercase, hyphens allowed)
  • URL validity checking
  • Enum value validation for status, category, layer
  • Cross-reference validation (compatibleWith, supersedes, supersededBy)
  • ID uniqueness validation within catalog

docs.title: Configurable Validator Options
docs.text:
• RequireImplementations - Require at least one implementation per standard
  • RequireDescription - Require descriptions for all standards
  • AllowedOrganizations - Restrict to specific organizations
  • AllowedCategories - Restrict to specific categories
  • AllowedLayers - Restrict to specific identity layers

docs.title: CLI Tool
docs.text:
The standards-catalog CLI provides five commands:

# Validate a catalog file
standards-catalog validate catalog.yaml

# List all standards in tabular format
standards-catalog list catalog.yaml

# Show details of a specific standard
standards-catalog show catalog.yaml oauth2

# Query with filters
standards-catalog query catalog.yaml --layer=agent --category=authorization

# Show catalog statistics
standards-catalog stats catalog.yaml

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/cmd/standards-catalog@v0.1.0

docs.title: Library Usage
docs.text:
import "github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/catalog"

// Load and validate
c, errors, err := catalog.LoadAndValidate("catalog.yaml")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
if errors.HasErrors() {
    log.Fatal(errors)
}

// Query standards
agentStandards := c.FindByLayer(catalog.LayerAgent)
authStandards := c.FindByCategory(catalog.CategoryAuthentication)

docs.title: Test Coverage
docs.text: Comprehensive unit tests with 96%+ code coverage.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository
  • Full Changelog

docs.title: v0.2.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-06-29

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Interactive D3.js web visualization for standards catalogs
  • New CLI commands: export and generate-web
  • Full MkDocs documentation site

docs.title: Web Visualization
docs.text:
Interactive browser-based visualization for exploring standards catalogs:

  • Graph View: Force-directed network showing standards and their relationships
  • Table View: Sortable table with all standard details
  • Filtering: Filter by layer, organization, category, and status
  • Color-coded: Standards colored by identity layer
  • Human: Cyan
  • Agent: Orange
  • Workload: Purple
  • Service: Green
  • Interactive: Click nodes to open specifications, drag to rearrange, zoom/pan

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export catalog as formatted JSON:

standards-catalog export catalog.yaml > catalog.json

docs.title: generate-web
docs.text:
Generate a standalone HTML visualization with embedded catalog data:

standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o standards-graph.html

The generated HTML file can be:

  • Opened directly in a browser
  • Embedded in MkDocs via iframe
  • Hosted on any static file server

docs.title: MkDocs Documentation Site
docs.text:
Full documentation site with Material theme:

  • Home page with overview and quick start
  • Getting Started guide
  • CLI Reference
  • Library Usage examples
  • Schema Reference
  • Interactive Visualization page
  • Changelog

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/standards-catalog-framework/cmd/standards-catalog@v0.2.0

docs.title: Generate Visualization
docs.text:
# Generate HTML visualization
standards-catalog generate-web catalog.yaml -o docs/standards-graph.html

# Export as JSON
standards-catalog export catalog.yaml > catalog.json

docs.title: Embed in MkDocs
docs.text:
<iframe src="standards-graph.html" width="100%" height="700" style="border:none;"></iframe>

Or link directly:

[View Standards Graph](standards-graph.html)
